body:
Title: CRA Form AB428 and Worksheet AB428 2025 Alberta resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5009-c/5009-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5009-c/5009-c-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5009-d/5009-d-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5009-d/5009-d-25e.pdf

CRA Form AB428 2025 calculates Alberta tax for Alberta residents. Part A calculates Alberta tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $60,000, $151,234, $181,481, $241,974, and $362,961. The rates are 8%, 10%, 12%, 13%, 14%, and 15%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $4,800.00, $13,923.40, $17,553.04, $25,417.13, and $42,355.31.

Part B calculates Alberta non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $22,323. Worksheet AB428 line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $6,221, starts reducing above $46,308, is zero at $87,782 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $22,323 for the spouse or common-law partner amount and eligible dependant amount after subtracting the spouse or dependant net income. Line 58360 pension income amount is capped at $1,719. Line 58440 disability amount for self is $17,219 unless the under-18 worksheet applies. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $2,884 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 8%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 60% and line 14 by 21%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Alberta non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Alberta tax. Line 61 adds Alberta tax on taxable income and Alberta tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet AB428 line 61520 calculates the Alberta dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 2.18% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 8.12%. Line 61540 multiplies federal minimum tax carryover line 40427 by 35%. Lines 65 and 66 subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover from tax and floor negative amounts at zero. Line 67 multiplies Form T691 Part 7 line 1 minus line 2 by 35%. Line 70 subtracts provincial foreign tax credit. Line 72 calculates the Alberta political contribution tax credit: contributions over $2,300 receive $1,000; otherwise the first $200 receives 75%, the next range to $1,100 receives 50% plus $150, and amounts over $1,100 receive 33.33% plus $600. Worksheet AB428 line 61545 calculates the Alberta supplemental tax credit by subtracting tuition from total claim amount, multiplying by 8%, subtracting $4,800, flooring at zero, and multiplying by 25%. Line 75 subtracts the supplemental tax credit and enters Alberta tax on line 42800 of the T1 return. Normalized numeric values encoded from AB428 and Worksheet AB428 include 60000, 151234, 181481, 241974, 362961, 0.08, 0.10, 0.12, 0.13, 0.14, 0.15, 4800, 13923.40, 17553.04, 25417.13, 42355.31, 22323, 6221, 46308, 87782, 1719, 17219, 2884, 0.03, 0.60, 0.21, 0.35, 0.0218, 0.0812, 200, 1100, 2300, 1000, 0.75, 0.50, 0.3333, 150, 600, and 0.25.
